chapter_goal: Compare elliptic and hyperbolic geometry by separating intrinsic quantities from model artifacts.

library_routing.matplotlib: durable static quotient, disk, isometry, and area diagrams
library_routing.numpy: projective, radial, Mobius, and distance computations
library_routing.plotly: interactive paired model-change artifact for corresponding Klein and Poincare points

proof_visualization_strategy: Use invariant trackers rather than copied proof diagrams: quotient distance, Lorentz/Klein distance, Poincare orthogonality, Mobius distance preservation, and area formula agreement.

source_span_read.source_map_note: pdf_page = printed_page + 9; pages were image-only for pdftotext and inspected from temporary renders

visual_sequence.concept: elliptic space as an antipodal sphere quotient
visual_sequence.inspection_target: a point has two spherical lifts but one elliptic identity
visual_sequence.validation: distance uses arccos(abs(dot)) and antipodal lifts have quotient distance zero

visual_sequence.concept: Klein chord versus Poincare circular arc
visual_sequence.inspection_target: the same geodesic is straight in Klein coordinates and curved in Poincare coordinates
visual_sequence.validation: radial map round-trip, chord-to-arc deviation, and boundary orthogonality residual

visual_sequence.concept: disk isometries preserve hyperbolic distance, not Euclidean placement
visual_sequence.inspection_target: points move and Euclidean distances change while Poincare distances stay fixed
visual_sequence.validation: pairwise hyperbolic distance residual after a disk automorphism

visual_sequence.concept: canonical area and model-dependent Euclidean radius
visual_sequence.inspection_target: equal hyperbolic radii occupy different Euclidean radii in Klein and Poincare charts
visual_sequence.validation: Klein and Poincare area formulas agree as functions of hyperbolic radius
